name: Freya Seed Lounge
title: Freya Seed Lounge
description: Curated seed community for Agora development, QA, and screenshots.
seo_description: Seeded community for onboarding, QA flows, and UI screenshots.
sidebar_md: A seeded Agora community with demo users, admins, challenges, and starter threads.
landing_intro_md: Welcome to the seeded demo community used for local development, onboarding tests, and screenshots.
faq_md:
## FAQ

### What is this?
A safe demo space for local testing.

### Can I post here?
Yes, this community is intended for smoke tests, UI reviews, and screenshots.
best_of_md:
- Invite flows
- Suggested community modules
- Challenge cards
- Notification journeys

rules.title: Stay constructive
rules.description: Treat this space as a product QA sandbox.

rules.title: Keep examples realistic
rules.description: Use posts that feel like real user activity.

wiki_pages.title: Getting Started
wiki_pages.body_md:
## Getting started

1. Join a community.
2. Create your first post.
3. Try save, vote, invite, and notifications.

challenge.title: Intro Week Prompt
challenge.prompt_md: Share one thing that would make your ideal online community feel safer and more welcoming.
challenge.share_text: I joined the Intro Week Prompt on Agora.

posts.title: Welcome to the seeded Agora workspace
posts.body_md: This thread explains the seeded content and points newcomers to invites, search, and notifications.

comments.body_md: Please use this thread as your anchor when checking the core product flows.
The onboarding + first post flow is much easier to test with a seeded thread like this.

posts.title: Best examples of community onboarding flows
posts.body_md: Collected inspiration for simple onboarding and habit-building patterns.

comments.body_md: The streak and encouragement patterns are especially relevant for our growth loops.

posts.title: Which seeded area should we polish next?
posts.body_md: Vote for the next area to tune in the seeded demo app.

name: Product Design Circle
title: Product Design Circle
description: Design systems, onboarding, UX writing, and interface polish.
seo_description: Design-focused Agora demo community for interface ideas and onboarding experiments.
sidebar_md: A seeded design community with warm critique, UI patterns, and product copy experiments.
landing_intro_md: Discuss interface polish, onboarding, retention, and community-first product design.
faq_md:
## FAQ

### What belongs here?
Interface systems, onboarding, writing, and accessibility discussions.
best_of_md:
- Empty-state reviews
- CTA experiments
- Mobile-first polish

rules.title: Be specific
rules.description: Critique the UI, not the person.

challenge.title: 7-minute Empty State Sprint
challenge.prompt_md: Draft a better empty state for a saved-posts screen. Make it warm, concrete, and action-oriented.
challenge.share_text: I joined the Empty State Sprint on Agora.

posts.title: What makes an onboarding flow feel genuinely welcoming?
posts.body_md: I am collecting examples of onboarding that feel helpful without becoming a tutorial maze.

comments.body_md: A good first-run flow should end with one meaningful action instead of five micro-steps.
I would also keep the language highly specific to the community people joined.

posts.title: Three empty states that should feel more alive
posts.body_md:
- Saved posts
- Notifications
- Invite landing pages

Which one should we polish first?

name: Slow Tech Club
title: Slow Tech Club
description: Calmer software, healthier defaults, and sustainable online spaces.
seo_description: Community for calmer product habits, humane technology, and sustainable defaults.
sidebar_md: A demo community about humane software, calm interfaces, and lower-stress digital habits.
landing_intro_md: Explore slower, calmer patterns for software and community design.
faq_md:
## FAQ

### Why slow tech?
Because calmer defaults can improve trust and retention.
best_of_md:
- Humane defaults
- Anti-spam patterns
- Responsible notifications

posts.title: Which product defaults reduce anxiety instead of increasing it?
posts.body_md: I am especially curious about notifications, invite prompts, and moderation cues.

comments.body_md: Soft, clear expectations help more than novelty. Good defaults can feel almost invisible.
